content.body: TIL that array and object literals weren't in the spec until ES3 (and trailing commas in object literals not until ES5)
wonder what browsers _actually_ did during that period though
content.formatted_body: wonder what browsers actually did during that period though

content.body: not sure about object literals but if you look at old old websites they do `new Array(values)` or assign values afterwards.
content.formatted_body: not sure about object literals but if you look at old old websites they do new Array(values) or assign values afterwards.

content.body: * not sure about object literals but if you look at old old websites they do `new Array(values)` or assign values afterwards, so i would imagine there were truly no array literals at the time.
